ABOUT ME

The human behind
the commits.

I'm Venu, a Full Stack Java Developer from Thane, Maharashtra. I build production-ready web applications end to end — designing RESTful APIs with Spring Boot, managing data with MySQL & PostgreSQL via Hibernate and JDBC, and delivering clean, responsive frontends with HTML · CSS · JavaScript.

I hold a BSc in Information Technology from Siddharth College (CGPA 7.5) and have shipped projects spanning secure banking systems to full-featured student management platforms. I write code that is readable, maintainable, and built to scale — and I'm actively looking for my first professional role in Java/Spring Boot development.

"Build for scale. Write for humans. Ship with confidence."

FEATURED WORK

Things I've shipped.

~/projects/upi-offline-mesh $ ./mvnw spring-boot:run LIVE DEMO
01 / Featured
📡 UPI Offline Mesh
A Spring Boot backend that demonstrates offline UPI-style payments routed through a Bluetooth-style gossip mesh — a phone encrypts a payment and broadcasts it device-to-device until one with connectivity bridges it to the backend, which decrypts, deduplicates, and settles it exactly once.
Java 17 Spring Boot 3 RSA + AES-GCM Concurrency H2
→ Hybrid RSA-2048 + AES-256-GCM encryption — intermediaries relay the packet but can't read it.
→ Gossip protocol simulator — packets hop phone-to-phone with TTL decay until a bridge node gets signal.
→ Exactly-once settlement under concurrent delivery, proven by a 3-thread idempotency race test.
Live Demo ↗ GitHub ↗
sender phone // offline, encrypts locally │ ▼ gossip (TTL hop) stranger-1 ──▶ stranger-2 ──▶ bridge │ walks outside, gets 4G ▼ POST /api/bridge/ingest spring boot backend ├─ hash ciphertext (SHA-256) ├─ claim hash idempotent ├─ decrypt + verify nonce └─ settle ledger ✓ exactly once
~/projects/fitness-tracker-microservices $ docker compose up --build
02 / Featured
🏋️ Fitness Tracker — Microservices
A six-service Spring Boot backend that turns a logged workout into AI-generated coaching. The activity service drops every workout onto a Kafka topic the instant it's saved, an AI service consumes it and asks Gemini what to do about it, and a gateway sits in front of everything enforcing real Keycloak OAuth2/PKCE — no shared database, no shortcuts.
Java 24 Spring Boot 3.5 Apache Kafka Keycloak OAuth2 Eureka + Config Server
→ Event-driven, not polled — activityservice publishes to Kafka the moment a workout is saved; aiservice consumes it and writes a Gemini-generated recommendation back.
→ Eureka handles service discovery and a Config Server centralizes every service's config — six moving parts, zero hardcoded URLs.
→ Polyglot persistence: PostgreSQL for user profiles, MongoDB for activities and AI recommendations, all gatekept by JWT validation at the gateway.
GitHub ↗
react frontend :5173 // vite + JWT │ ▼ POST /api/activities api gateway :8080 ──validates JWT──▶ keycloak │ ├──▶ user-service :8081 ──▶ PostgreSQL │ └──▶ activity-service :8082 ──▶ MongoDB │ publish ▼ kafka topic: activity-events │ consume ▼ ai-service :8083 ──▶ Gemini API └─ recommendation ✓ stored in MongoDB // registry & shared config for all five services eureka :8761 config-server :8888
